app.name: NeuroSustain
dashboard.title: Cognitive Dashboard
dashboard.firstTimeDesc: Start your first session to establish your cognitive baseline.
dashboard.radarEmpty: Complete exercises to see your cognitive profile
dashboard.historyEmpty: Training history requires at least 3 active days to unlock neural trend analysis. Keep pushing!
train.title: Training Exercises
train.storm.title: NEURAL STORM
train.storm.desc: Chaotic 3-minute mixed mode. Rapid exercise switching to maximize cognitive flexibility. No FSRS impact.
exercise.reaction.name: Reaction Time
exercise.reaction.description: Press SPACE the instant the circle turns green. Measures your raw processing speed.
exercise.reaction.instruction: Press SPACE when the circle turns green
exercise.reaction.missed: Too slow! Try to react faster.
exercise.stroop.name: Stroop Task
exercise.stroop.description: Identify the color of the text, not the word itself. Trains inhibitory control.
results.title: Session Complete
profile.title: Profile
glicko.tooltip: Glicko-2 Skill Rating. RD indicates uncertainty (lower is more accurate).
insight.attention: 20 minutes of passive scrolling can reduce sustained attention capacity for up to 2 hours. This session is designed to recalibrate your prefrontal cortex.
insight.sleep: Your reaction time consistency reflects cognitive freshness. Studies (DOI: 10.1093/sleep/zsaa170) show even 1 hour of sleep debt increases reaction time variability by 15%.
insight.exercise: A 10-minute walk before cognitive training increases BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor), which enhances synaptic plasticity during exercises like these.
insight.hydration: Dehydration as mild as 2% body weight loss impairs working memory and attention. Your cognitive performance today may benefit from a glass of water.
insight.dual: Dual-task exercises like N-Back are the only computerized training shown to transfer to fluid intelligence (DOI: 10.1073/pnas.0801268105).
insight.neuroplasticity: Your brain forms approximately 700 new neurons daily in the hippocampus. Cognitive training helps integrate these neurons into functional circuits.
insight.aiDependency: Relying on AI for semantic synthesis reduces activation in Broca's area. Exercises like Semantic Linker force your prefrontal cortex to rebuild those pathways.
insight.contextSwitch: The average person's context-switching cost is 400ms of cognitive "dead time." Training reduces this refractory period, building resilience to digital fragmentation.
insight.prefrontal: The prefrontal cortex — your brain's CEO — thins measurably after months of high-scroll behavior. These exercises force the Dorsolateral PFC to rebuild executive control pathways.
insight.inhibition: Scrolling is a "Go-Go-Go" behavior — always swiping, always clicking. Exercises with No-Go signals (red circles, fakeout tones) strengthen the neural brakes needed to stop scrolling and put the phone down.
insight.deepWork: Short-form video trains your brain to switch contexts every 15 seconds. Exercises like Serial Subtraction stretch that window back to 2-3 minutes of sustained single-context focus.
insight.brainDrain: The mere presence of your smartphone reduces available cognitive capacity (Ward et al., 2017). Consider placing your phone in another room during training.
calibration.intro.title: Hardware Calibration
calibration.intro.instruction: Click the target {count} times as fast as you can to establish your hardware noise floor.
calibration.warning.fair: Hardware latency detected (±{ms}ms jitter). Focus on trend data rather than absolute millisecond values.
calibration.warning.poor: High hardware latency detected. Absolute reaction time values may be offset by up to one display frame (≈16ms). Trend comparisons remain valid.
fatigue.toast.title: Cognitive Fatigue Detected
fatigue.toast.desc: EMA +{rise}% above baseline ({base}ms → {curr}ms). Consider finishing this session and resting.
exercise.highNumber.name: High Number
exercise.highNumber.description: Select the numerically highest value — ignore the physical size. Trains inhibitory control against visual interference.
exercise.serialSub.name: Serial Subtraction
exercise.serialSub.description: Subtract repeatedly from a starting number. The previous answer disappears — hold it in working memory.
exercise.pianoPlayer.name: Piano Player
exercise.pianoPlayer.description: Listen to the sequence of tones and repeat it. Trains auditory and sequential working memory.
exercise.fallacy.name: Fallacy Detector
exercise.fallacy.description: Classify logical arguments as Valid or Fallacy. Trains critical thinking and inhibition of emotional reasoning.
exercise.hanoi.name: Tower of Hanoi
exercise.hanoi.description: Move all discs from peg 1 to peg 3 without placing a larger disc on a smaller one. Trains forward-thinking and sustained planning.
exercise.setSwitch.name: Set Switching
exercise.setSwitch.description: Match cards based on the active rule (Color or Shape). Trains cognitive flexibility.
exercise.changeMaker.name: Change Maker
exercise.changeMaker.description: Calculate exact change using a cash tray. Trains mental arithmetic and cognitive flexibility.
exercise.wordScramble.name: Word Scramble
exercise.wordScramble.description: Unscramble the letters to form a word. Trains linguistic flexibility and mental rotation.
exercise.blockCount3d.name: 3D Box Counting
exercise.blockCount3d.description: Calculate the total number of cubes in the 3D structure, including hidden ones. Trains spatial visualization.
exercise.nback.name: Dual N-Back
exercise.nback.description: Track audio and visual stimuli from N steps ago. The gold standard for working memory.
exercise.patternBreaker.name: Pattern Breaker
exercise.patternBreaker.description: Find the subtle anomaly in a dense repeating pattern. Trains sustained visual attention and resistance to visual fatigue.
exercise.symbolSearch.name: Symbol Search
exercise.symbolSearch.description: Find all matching symbols in the grid as fast as possible. Measures raw perceptual scanning speed.
exercise.inspectionTime.name: The Flash
exercise.inspectionTime.description: Three shapes flash briefly — one changes. Identify which one. Measures raw visual intake speed.
